title: porcelia mediocris
intro: Porcelia mediocris, also known as Maypop or Wild Passionflower, is a perennial plant native to the eastern United States and Canada. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant has been found to have health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. Herbal preparations of Porcelia mediocris include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
